Fear and Greed is at
24
(Fear).
The market has quietly recovered from the cycle low hit last
week, with BTC climbing back toward
$66K
over the weekend as selling pressure eased.

The macro picture is stabilising. But the news landscape shifted
dramatically over the weekend. The US government pulled
Anthropic's two most advanced AI models with an emergency
directive. SpaceX's tokenized IPO bids collapsed on three
exchanges. And Saylor gave his clearest explanation yet for why
Strategy sold Bitcoin.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

US Orders Anthropic To Pull Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5

The US government issued an emergency export control directive
on June 13 ordering Anthropic to suspend Claude Fable 5 and
Claude Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals on national security
grounds. To comply, Anthropic disabled both models globally.

The models had been released just days earlier. All other
Anthropic models remain accessible.

What capability of Mythos Preview made these models a national
security concern?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/mZew1wb7wET3qB5BSlYrcA~~/AAQRxRA~/jLq8tV5HD1KZBaz0HEeKfFL4BKJDzOfX3s61AbVYKC6IGi_BAaAl5nzGZoKptsGDpjCSkeidMhZD2t8Ev8Fhn0QCSmsmzqU9siGWbI0xm06iM1HHFQ3sbjsMq-hg3oOoaDCyCdRMNTEHc6krlJsrbAMY5xvQPhWwU9ESqoj2Uos~ )
    Saylor Says Bitcoin Sales Are Core to Strategy's Business

Michael Saylor said at the BTC Prague conference that
S

74538.subject: Avalanche Ecosystem Analysis: Consolidation with an Accumulation Bias
74538.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/avalanche-ecosystem-analysis-consolidation

Most people only notice a blockchain when it becomes loud. When DEX volume accelerates, memecoins run, gas fees rise, and the timeline starts repeating the same narrative, it becomes easy to say that an ecosystem is вАЬback.вАЭ
Avalanche is moving through a quieter phase right now, but the underlying network picture is more stable than the surface activity suggests.
The latest Nansen AI snapshot shows a network that is still dealing with muted speculation, low fees, and cautious Smart Money behavior. But beneath that quieter surface, several core signals have improved. AVAX exchange outflows are significant, fresh wallet inflows have surged, sAVAX continues to show demand, BTC.b is seeing exchange outflows, and Avalanche L1 activity remains meaningful.
At first glance, that can look discouraging, but it is also not a clean distribution phase either. The better read is that Avalanche remains in consolidation, now with a stronger accumulation bias than the prior snapshot.
All onchain figures in this article are based on the Nansen AI and DeFiLlama snapshot provided for June 11, 2026 and should be read as a point-in-time analysis rather than a permanent state of the Avalanche ecosystem. This is not financial advice, and it is not a call to buy or sell AVAX. It is a data-driven look at what the network is showing beneath the surface.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
The Network is Quieter
The current network data shows that Avalanche is not in a high-fee, high-speculation cycle. Chain fees remain very low, with 24-hour chain fees and revenue at $2,068. That tells us blockspace demand is not intense right now.
At the same time, the data points to a network that is very much alive and scaling. The latest snapshot shows 735,756 daily active addresses, 37 transactions per second, 14.1B all-time transactions, 81.6M contracts deployed, and 81 active Avalanche L1s.
DeFi activity is also still present, though not accelerating aggressively. Avalanche TVL is currently around $455.02M, with $1.412B in stablecoin market cap and $84.48M in 24h DEX volume.
The surface read is clear. This is not a network relying on noise. It is a network with a real activity base, steady usage, and infrastructure that continues to compound beneath the marketвАЩs attention.
USDC Flows are the Strongest Constructive Signal
Stablecoin behavior remains one of the most important areas to watch.
In the latest snapshot, USDC showed -$6.5M in exchange outflows over 24h and -$2.7M over 7d. Fresh wallets saw +$20.2M in USDC inflows over 24h, about 2.3x the average. Top PnL wallets also showed +$3.3M in USDC inflows over 7d, around 3.9x above average.
This does not automatically mean aggressive risk-on deployment. Stablecoins can move for many reasons: self-custody, DeFi positioning, treasu

Happy Sunday dear frogs!
LetвАЩs get after it!

And as always make sure to follow Mantle on Twitter/X for more updates [ https://substack.com/redirect/f7f509cd-aaa2-477a-b4d9-459c49900866?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and stay tuned as we continue shining a light on all the cool things they are doing!

рЯІ† AI's Crypto News Brief: A July 1 MiCA cutoff looms for millions of EU crypto users while a DPRK-linked $36M hack and a UK naval seizure exposing USDT sanctions evasion show that crypto's geopolitical risk is no longer theoretical. рЯЪ® Millions of EU Crypto Users Face Exchange Cutoff as MiCA Deadline Hits in Days On July 1, 2026, the temporary operating permission that allowed crypto companies to serve EU customers while awaiting a full MiCA license expires вАФ and exchanges, brokers, and wallet services that have not secured authorization must immediately cut off access to European users with no further grace period. CryptoSlate reports that the deadline creates a live operational crisis for ordinary investors who may lose access to their funds, positions, or platforms overnight, with little notice from providers scrambling to reach compliance. Read full story рЯТА Humanity Protocol's $36M Hack Linked to Suspected North Korean Hackers Cybersecurity firm Quantstamp has attributed the $36 million breach of Humanity Protocol to suspected North Korean threat actors, identifying a fraudulent phishing email spoofing crypto exchange Bithumb as the attack's primary entry vector. The incident adds another eight-figure sum to the growing ledger of assets stolen by DPRK-affiliated hacking groups, which have systematically targeted blockchain projects to fund state programs operating under international sanctions. More details вЪЦпЄП CFTC No-Action Letter Opens First Legal Path for Digital Commodity Perpetuals in the US The CFTC issued a staff no-action letter that clears a legal runway for perpetual futures contracts on digital commodities to operate within the United States вАФ a product category that has until now existed almost exclusively at offshore venues to sidestep regulatory uncertainty. The guidance provides immediate operational clarity for US-domiciled exchanges and their institutional clients, who have long been unable to offer the perpetual swap structures that dominate global crypto derivatives volumes.
